title: T-002 — license_plates table + RLS + composite FEFO index (WH §5.2)
prompt:
# T-002 — license_plates table + RLS + composite FEFO index (WH §5.2)

PRD: docs/prd/05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md §5.2
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create the `license_plates` physical table — the atomic inventory unit per ADR-001 — with all 30+ columns from §5.2, RLS on org_id via foundation app.current_org_id(), partial-unique reservation constraint, and the composite FEFO index used by §9.2.

## Implementation contract
1. New migration `0NN_license_plates.sql` creating `license_plates(...)` per PRD §5.2 column list, including dual-UoM (`quantity` + `uom` + `catch_weight_kg`), state (`status`, `qa_status`), batch (`batch_number`, `supplier_batch_number`, `gtin`), expiry (`expiry_date`, `shelf_life_mode_snapshot`, `date_code_rendered`), lineage (`grn_id`, `wo_id`, `parent_lp_id`, `reserved_for_wo_id`, `consumed_by_wo_id`), locking (`locked_by`, `locked_at`), and JSONB extensions (`ext_jsonb`, `private_jsonb` per ADR-028).
2. Constraints: `UNIQUE(org_id, warehouse_id, lp_number)`; `CHECK (quantity >= 0)`; `CHECK (reserved_qty <= quantity)`; partial unique `UNIQUE (lp_id) WHERE status IN ('available','reserved')` (deferred to T-004 for cross-table reservation enforcement).
3. ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY + policy `license_plates_org_context` USING (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) WITH CHECK (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) — the foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC.
4. Composite FEFO index: `(org_id, warehouse_id, product_id, status, expiry_date ASC NULLS LAST)` per §5.2.
5. Drizzle schema file `packages/db/src/schema/license-plates.ts` mirroring columns; export typed Insert/Select.

## Files
- packages/db/src/schema/license-plates.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_license_plates.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/license-plates.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when `\d license_plates` and `pg_policies` are inspected, then every PRD §5.2 column exists with the documented type and nullability, RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY are enabled, and the license_plates policy qual/withcheck reference `app.current_org_id()` with no `current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id')` GUC.
2. Given two orgs insert rows and the connection as `app_user` has called `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_A)`, when `SELECT * FROM license_plates` runs, then only org A rows are visible (RLS enforced via `app.current_org_id()`).
3. Given an LP with quantity=100, when `UPDATE license_plates SET reserved_qty = 150` runs, then it fails the CHECK constraint.
4. Given the FEFO composite index exists, when `EXPLAIN` runs the §9.2 FEFO query, then the planner uses the composite index (Index Scan, not Seq Scan).

## Test strategy
- RED: `packages/db/src/schema/license-plates.test.ts` asserting RLS isolation + CHECK constraints + composite index presence via `pg_indexes` lookup.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test license-plates`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not read `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` directly — use the foundation `app.current_org_id()` function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
- Do not use `tenant_id` as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is `org_id`.
- Do not omit RLS / FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY — every org table is RLS-mandatory per ADR-003/013.
- Do not skip the composite FEFO index (§9.2 SLO <500 ms depends on it).
- Do not add cross-table FKs (lp_reservations, lp_genealogy) in this migration — they live in T-003/T-004.
- Do not store `lp_number` without per-warehouse uniqueness (constraint required).

## Dependencies
- T-001

pipeline_inputs.details: Create the `license_plates` physical table — the atomic inventory unit per ADR-001 — with all 30+ columns from §5.2 (business-scope column is `org_id` per Wave0 v4.3), RLS on `org_id = app.current_org_id()` (foundation function) + FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY, partial-unique reservation constraint, and the composite FEFO index used by §9.2. New migration `0NN_license_plates.sql` creating `license_plates(...)` per PRD §5.2 column list, including dual-UoM (`quantity` + `uom` + `catch_weight_kg`), state (`status`, `qa_status`), batch (`batch_number`, `supplier_batch_number`, `gtin`), expiry (`expiry_date`, `shelf_life_mode_snapshot`, `date_code_rendered`), lineage (`grn_id`, `wo_id`, `parent_lp_id`, `reserved_for_wo_id`, `consumed_by_wo_id`), locking (`locked_by`, `locked_at`), and JSONB extensions (`ext_jsonb`, `private_jsonb` per ADR-028). Constraints: `UNIQUE(org_id, warehouse_id, lp_number)`; `CHECK (quantity >= 0)`; `CHECK (reserved_qty <= quantity)`; partial unique `UNIQUE (lp_id) WHERE status IN ('available','reserved')` (deferred to T-004 for cross-table reservation enforcement). RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context). AC count consolidated from 5→4 by Fixer F6 2026-05-14 (no coverage lost; the pg_policies/app.current_org_id() text assertion was fused into AC1 and remains explicitly tested by the test_strategy pg_policies check).
out_of_scope: No FK to grns/work_orders (kept nullable in this task)
acceptance_criteria: Given the migration runs, when `\d license_plates` and `pg_policies` are inspected, then every PRD §5.2 column exists with the documented type and nullability, org_id is NOT NULL, RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY are enabled, and the license_plates policy qual/withcheck reference app.current_org_id() with no current_setting('app.tenant_id'|'app.current_org_id') GUC.

Given two orgs insert rows and the app_user connection has called app.set_org_context(session_token, org_A), when `SELECT * FROM license_plates` runs, then only org A rows are visible (RLS enforced via app.current_org_id()).
Given an LP with quantity=100, when `UPDATE license_plates SET reserved_qty = 150` runs, then it fails the CHECK constraint.
Given the FEFO composite index exists, when `EXPLAIN` runs the §9.2 FEFO query, then the planner uses the composite index (Index Scan, not Seq Scan).

Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test license-plates`.

Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id.
Do not omit RLS/FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY — every org table is RLS-mandatory per ADR-003/013.
Do not skip the composite FEFO index (§9.2 SLO <500 ms depends on it).
Do not add cross-table FKs (lp_reservations, lp_genealogy) in this migration — they live in T-003/T-004.
Do not store `lp_number` without per-warehouse uniqueness (constraint required).
